key_insight: CO₂ laser welding of 2 mm Strenx 700 CR steel can produce sound joints with a narrow heat-affected zone, but it significantly alters local microstructure toward harder phases such as martensite and bainite.
engineering_relevance: For lightweight agricultural machinery components, laser welding offers a path to high-strength, fast fabrication with potentially better fatigue performance than conventional arc welding.
method: Autogenous CO₂ laser welding at 1100, 1300, and 1500 W
Parallel-edge joints without root opening on 2 mm cold-rolled Strenx 700 sheets
Post-weld macro/microstructure, microhardness, tensile, and tension-tension fatigue evaluation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports satisfactory weld formation and phase mapping across the fusion zone and heat-affected zones using optical/electron microscopy, hardness mapping, tensile tests, and uniaxial fatigue testing.
watch_item: Higher laser power can increase local thermal cycling and hard-phase formation, so fatigue behavior and cracking risk must be validated before industrial deployment.
paper_title: Effects of Laser Welding on the Microstructure and Fatigue Behavior of Strenx 700 CR Steel
